original_prompt:
Build me something like ordinal. I want to copy their feature set. Here is the link: 

https://www.tryordinal.com


## Change requested

The current app misinterpreted Ordinal. VERIFIED feature set from the live site (https://www.tryordinal.com — fetched 2026-07-10): Ordinal is "a powerful end-to-end platform to draft, plan, and schedule social media content" for social media managers, agencies, and growth teams. Core modules: (1) Scheduling & posting — draft posts, cross-post to multiple channels from one composer, post previews, recurring posts; (2) Planning — rich content calendar with campaign management, ideas/drafts section, collaboration + approval workflows; (3) Auto-engagement — scheduled auto-likes/comments/reposts and "post is live" notifications; (4) Analytics — daily reporting on followers/engagement/impressions, insights filterable by post type and content label.

Rework this app toward that real shape while REUSING what exists: keep Campaign, Asset, the Draft → In Review → Approved / Needs Rework state machine (it maps directly to Ordinal's approval workflow), versioning, and status history. ADD, as local-first single-user features with simulated (never real) network activity:
1. POST as a first-class object: caption text, target channels (Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, LinkedIn), attached asset(s), per-channel preview cards, scheduled date/time. A post must reach Approved before it can be scheduled.
2. Content calendar screen: month view of scheduled/published posts, color-coded by channel; tap to open post.
3. Publish simulation: at/after the scheduled time (or via a "Publish now (simulated)" button), the post transitions Scheduled → Published with a fake permalink and timestamp in its history. No real network calls, no credentials — clearly label everything "Simulated".
4. Ideas/drafts inbox: quick-capture list that can be promoted to a full post.
5. Analytics screen: mock per-channel metrics (followers, impressions, engagement) as simple charts, filterable by channel and content label; label posts with free-form content labels.
6. Recurring posts: a repeat rule (weekly/biweekly/monthly) that materializes the next occurrence when one publishes.
Keep every existing hard requirement: offline, SwiftData persistence, real empty/loading/error states, accessibility, no real platform integrations. The app must compile cleanly for the iOS Simulator with the MarketingCampaignCockpit scheme.

phase_discussions.title: Scope the change
phase_discussions.output:
Codex's file-level plan is thorough and technically sound — reusing `ReviewStateMachine`/`ReviewStatus` for Post's approval gate instead of inventing a parallel one, and modeling channels/attachments relationally instead of transformable arrays, are both the right calls to avoid schema churn. I'll adopt that plan as the baseline. Where I'll overrule/tighten it: the approval gate has to be structural (can't literally select a schedule date/time unless `status == .approved`), not a UI warning; every simulated artifact (permalink, publish action, auto-engagement, analytics numbers) needs the word "Simulated" inline next to it, not in a footnote; recurrence has to be computed synchronously at publish-time (real or simulated), never timer-driven, since there's no real background clock to trust in a local-only app; and auto-engagement gets folded into Post detail as a couple of toggles that write a visible history event ("Auto-like scheduled for 2h post-publish — Simulated") rather than becoming its own subsystem — it's the weakest-linked feature and doesn't earn separate screens/models. Analytics stays intentionally small: mock per-channel followers/impressions/engagement with channel + label filters, simple native Charts, no drill-down beyond that.

CONSENSUS: YES

## Final Output

**Reuse, unchanged:** `ReviewStateMachine.swift`, `ReviewStatus.swift`, `Asset.swift`, `AssetRepository.swift`, `ReviewEvent.swift` (only additive post-linkage fields). Campaign's transition table, versioning, and approval semantics do not change.

**New domain/data:**
- `Post` (SwiftData model): `caption`, `contentLabel`, `targetChannels` (relational/enum join, no transformable arrays), `scheduledFor`, `publishedAt`, `fakePermalink`, `recurrenceRule` (none/weekly/biweekly/monthly), `isSimulated`, `campaign` relationship, asset attachment relation, and the *existing* `ReviewStatus` for its approval state (no second state machine).
- `PostRepository`: `createQuickCapture`, `saveDraft`, `applyTransition` (via `ReviewStateMachine`), `setTargets`, `schedule` (rejects unless `.approved`), `publishNowSimulated`, `publishIfDue(now:)`, `materializeNextOccurrence(postID:)`.
- `PostPublishingCoordinator` (CockpitServices): scans due posts, publishes with fake permalink + timestamp as a history event, and synchronously materializes the next recurrence at that same moment — never on a background timer.
- `ReviewEventKind` additions: `postScheduled`, `postPublished`, `postRecurred`, `autoEngagementSimulated`, `simulatedPublishingFailed`.
- `ReviewEventSnapshot`/`ReviewEventRepository`: add `postID`, `events(forPostID:)`.
- Schema container: register `Post` + attachment-link model.

**App shell:** `AppEnvironment`, `MarketingCampaignCockpitApp` (inject `PostRepository`/coordinator, run startup due-post sweep + on-`.active` sweep), `Route` (add `postList`, `postComposer`, `postDetail`), `HistoryScope` (add `.post`), `RootView` (tab shell: Campaigns, Calendar, Ideas, Analytics), `StatusHistoryViewModel` + `HistoryEntryRow` (post event labels).

**New UI (Features/Posts/):** `PostInboxView` (quick-capture + promote), `PostComposerView` (caption/label/channels/assets/preview cards; schedule controls structurally disabled until Approved; recurrence picker; "Simulated" labeling throughout), `PostCalendarView` (month grid, color-by-channel, tap-through), `PostDetailView` (edit, per-channel previews, "Publish now (Simulated)" button, auto-engagement toggles writing history events, history link), `PostAnalyticsView` (channel/label filters, mock Charts), `PostHubViewModel` + per-screen view models with empty/loading/error states.

**Tests:** extend `ModelIntegrityTests` (Post + recurrence round-trip) and `RepositoryTests` (create/schedule/publish/recur, including the approval gate rejecting an unapproved schedule attempt).

**Priority if time-constrained:** Post + gated scheduling + Calendar are non-negotiable; Publish simulation and Ideas inbox ship in full (they're cheap); Analytics and Recurrence are the ones to trim first if needed, but trim scope (not correctness) — e.g. fewer chart types, coarser recurrence granularity — never skip the "Simulated" labeling or the structural approval gate.

**Claude (Product Strategist · Skeptic) — Round 1**

Given the sandbox doesn't expose `app_build` to me directly in this phase, I'll rely on the documented existing state (Campaign, Asset, Draft→In Review→Approved/Needs Rework, versioning, status history) as described in the prompt and give my independent take.

My read: this is a good correction, but I want to be the person in the room who asks whether we're actually fixing the misunderstanding or just piling six new nouns onto a Campaign/Asset app and calling it Ordinal. The core problem Ordinal solves is "I run social accounts for other people/brands and need one place to draft, get sign-off, and get things onto a calendar without missing a slot." The object that was missing wasn't a feature, it was the unit of work — a Post. Everything else (calendar, publish sim, analytics, recurrence) is a view or a lifecycle event on top of Post. If I don't nail Post as the spine, I'll end up with six loosely-connected screens that each simulate a different toy, which is worse than what we started with.

So my strongest direction: make Post a SwiftData model that composes what already exists rather than duplicating it — Post has channels, caption, scheduledDate, a reference to existing Asset(s), and reuses the existing Campaign approval state machine verbatim instead of inventing a second one. If Campaign already carries Draft→In Review→Approved/Needs Rework with history, Post should point at that same enum/type, not a parallel copy-pasted one with slightly different case names. That reuse is the difference between "reworked toward the real shape" and "bolted a demo onto the side." I'd resist the temptation to give Post its own richer state machine just because it has more real-world states (scheduled, published) — those are additive states that come *after* Approved, not a replacement approval flow.

Biggest risk to my own position: six requested features (Post, calendar, publish-sim, ideas inbox, analytics, recurrence) is a lot for one iteration that's supposed to stay minimal. If we try to do all six with equal depth we'll get five shallow screens and one crash. I'd rather explicitly rank them: Post + approval-gated scheduling + calendar are the load-bearing trio that actually make this "Ordinal-shaped" — those are non-negotiable. Publish simulation is cheap (a button, a state transition, a fake permalink string) and should ship fully. Ideas inbox is cheap (a list + a "promote to post" action reusing the Post creation flow). Analytics and recurring posts are the two I'd flag as the most likely to get half-built: analytics because "mock charts filterable by channel and label" can quietly become a whole mini-BI feature, and recurrence because "materializes the next occurrence when one publishes" requires a background-ish trigger in a local-only app with no real clock/push — it has to be evaluated synchronously at publish-time (simulated or manual), not on a timer, or it will either not fire in the simulator or require fake scheduling infrastructure we don't need.

The detail I don't want lost in synthesis: "a post must reach Approved before it can be scheduled" is a hard gate, not a UI hint — the calendar and the composer both need to enforce it structurally (e.g., you literally cannot pick a scheduled date/time until status == .approved), not just show a warning label, otherwise it's decorative and someone will "verify" it works when it doesn't actually block anything. One thing I'd push back on in the ask itself: "auto-engagement — scheduled auto-likes/comments/reposts" is the single feature here with the weakest connection to the Campaign/Asset/Post spine and the highest risk of being pure decoration (a toggle that does nothing observable). If we're being honest about scope, I'd rather fold it into the Post detail view as a couple of simulated settings that produce a visible history entry ("Auto-like scheduled for 2h post-publish — Simulated") than build it as a separate subsystem. If the room disagrees and wants it as a first-class thing, fine, but I want that tradeoff on the record before we commit engineering time to it.

I extracted that block into its own `@ViewBuilder private func assetsSection(for campaign: Campaign) -> some View`, called as `assetsSection(for: campaign)` from the single `if let campaign = ...` in the main form. This mirrors the working pattern already used for the same `ForEach(visibleAssets, id: \.id)` construct in `CampaignDetailView.swift`, and matches the existing convention in this file of factoring nested builder content into helper methods (`form(viewModel:)`, `reviewActions(for:viewModel:)`). No behavior changed — same rows, same tap-to-toggle logic.
❌ **Verification after repair 1** — compile FAILED for the iOS Simulator (xcodebuild)

**Repair 2 (Claude)**

I can't get interactive approval to run `xcodebuild` in this session, so I verified the fix by static analysis instead of a live build.

**Root cause**: When the `ForEach(visibleAssets, id: \.id) { asset in ... }` block was extracted from inline `Form` content into a separate `@ViewBuilder private func assetsSection(for:) -> some View`, the opaque `some View` return type gave the compiler less contextual type information for generic inference. Swift's overload resolution for `ForEach` then mis-picked the `ForEach(_ data: Binding<C>, id:, content: (Binding<C.Element>) -> Content)` overload instead of the plain-array overload, which is why `asset` was being treated as a `Binding<Asset>` and `asset.id` came out as `Binding<Subject>` (not `UUID`) at the `toggleAsset(asset.id)` call site.

**Fix** (`MarketingCampaignCockpit/Features/Posts/PostComposerView.swift:271-289`): made both the array's element type and the closure's parameter type explicit so the compiler can no longer consider the `Binding<C>` overload:
- `let visibleAssets: [Asset] = campaign.assets.filter { ... }`
- `ForEach(visibleAssets, id: \Asset.id) { (asset: Asset) in ... }`

This forces resolution to the plain-array `ForEach(_:id:content:)` initializer, matching how it type-checked before the refactor.
❌ **Verification after repair 2** — compile FAILED for the iOS Simulator (xcodebuild)

## Coordinator Decision

See the coordinator's message above.

## Final Output

That commit will be handled by the orchestrator's own commit step, so I don't need to run it manually. My integration check is complete — here's the recap.

## Final Output

Looking at this phase's discussion, the picture is clear: the design/scope pivot is genuinely good (Post as the spine object reusing the existing approval state machine, structural gating on scheduling, inline "Simulated" labeling, synchronous recurrence), but the app does not compile. That fact has to be the headline of this review, not a caveat buried under praise for the architecture. Two repair attempts on `PostComposerView.swift` gave two different, contradictory root-cause explanations for the same `ForEach`/`Binding` type-inference error, and neither was verified against actual compiler output — both were guesses against a symptom. The Product Strategist's point stands: nobody has actually run `xcodebuild` and read the real diagnostic text yet, so it's possible the reported error isn't even the real blocker (Swift's type-checker often stops surfacing accurate detail after the first failure in a file), and the "field-for-field" naming reconciliation between the UI and data layers was a manual trace, not a compiler-confirmed match.

CONSENSUS: YES

## Final Output

**What was built:** A real pivot from the old Campaign/Asset-only cockpit toward Ordinal's actual shape. `Post` is a new SwiftData model that reuses the existing `ReviewStatus`/`ReviewStateMachine` for approval (no parallel state machine) and adds caption, content label, target channels, asset attachments, scheduling, recurrence, and simulated-publish fields. `PostRepository` implements quick-capture, drafting, gated scheduling (`schedule()` throws unless `.approved` — a structural gate, not a UI hint), simulated publish with a fake permalink + history event, and synchronous recurrence materialization at publish time. `PostPublishingCoordinator` sweeps due posts on launch and foreground (never a background timer). Five new screens — Ideas inbox, Composer, Calendar, Detail, Analytics — sit behind a 4-tab `RootView`, using shared `SimulatedBadge`/`ChannelChip`/`PostPreviewCard` components so every fake artifact is labeled "Simulated" inline. Status history understands the new post-scoped event kinds. On paper this covers all six requested features plus the reuse constraints.

**Verification status: FAILED.** `xcodebuild` for the iOS Simulator did not compile, and it still failed after two repair attempts. The two repairs disagreed with each other about the root cause of the same error in `PostComposerView.swift` (one blamed excessive `ForEach` nesting and extracted a helper function; the other blamed opaque-return-type inference loss and reverted to explicit inline types) — that pattern indicates guessing against a reported symptom, not a diagnosis grounded in real compiler output, since no repair attempt in this history had approval to actually run and read `xcodebuild`'s output. Right now there is no working app to hand a user.

**Prompt coverage (design-level, not yet running):** Post as first-class object with channels/assets/scheduling — done. Content calendar screen — done. Publish simulation with fake permalink/timestamp — done. Ideas/drafts inbox — done. Analytics screen with channel/label filters — done. Recurring posts materializing at publish — done. Approval-gated scheduling — done structurally. All of this is credited as real design/implementation progress, but none of it is verified functional because the build doesn't compile.

**Known limitations (scope trade-offs, not bugs, to be stated plainly once it builds):** recurrence only materializes when the app is foregrounded or launched (no true background execution — correct for a local-only app, but a user could expect it to "just happen"); auto-engagement is two toggles plus a history line, not a real subsystem; analytics numbers are static mocks, not live-feeling data; the integrator's cross-file name matching (`Post`, `Channel`, `RecurrenceRule`, `PostRepository` methods) was a manual trace, not compiler-confirmed, so residual naming mismatches elsewhere in the codebase are still possible.

**Top follow-ups, ranked:**
1. **Launch blocker, owner needed:** get explicit approval to run `xcodebuild build -project MarketingCampaignCockpit.xcodeproj -scheme MarketingCampaignCockpit -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16'` (and `swift test` for the two packages), capture the actual compiler output, and fix the specific error it reports — not a third blind guess at `PostComposerView`. This is the single concrete next step and it has no owner yet.
2. Once compiling, re-run the manual interface trace as an actual test pass (`ModelIntegrityTests`, `RepositoryTests` including the schedule-requires-approval rejection case) to convert the integrator's static confidence into verified confidence.
3. Do a pass through the five new screens for empty/loading/error states and accessibility (VoiceOver labels on channel toggles, calendar day cells, chart data points) — flagged as required in an earlier quality gate but never explicitly re-confirmed against the finished screens.

**Bottom line:** No-go on "ready to run" today — verification is FAILED and that has to be the headline. The scope and architecture decisions are sound and should carry forward unchanged into the next build attempt; what's missing is someone actually reading real compiler output instead of guessing at `PostComposerView` a third time.

phase_outputs.iterate_scope:
Codex's file-level plan is thorough and technically sound — reusing `ReviewStateMachine`/`ReviewStatus` for Post's approval gate instead of inventing a parallel one, and modeling channels/attachments relationally instead of transformable arrays, are both the right calls to avoid schema churn. I'll adopt that plan as the baseline. Where I'll overrule/tighten it: the approval gate has to be structural (can't literally select a schedule date/time unless `status == .approved`), not a UI warning; every simulated artifact (permalink, publish action, auto-engagement, analytics numbers) needs the word "Simulated" inline next to it, not in a footnote; recurrence has to be computed synchronously at publish-time (real or simulated), never timer-driven, since there's no real background clock to trust in a local-only app; and auto-engagement gets folded into Post detail as a couple of toggles that write a visible history event ("Auto-like scheduled for 2h post-publish — Simulated") rather than becoming its own subsystem — it's the weakest-linked feature and doesn't earn separate screens/models. Analytics stays intentionally small: mock per-channel followers/impressions/engagement with channel + label filters, simple native Charts, no drill-down beyond that.

CONSENSUS: YES

## Final Output

**Reuse, unchanged:** `ReviewStateMachine.swift`, `ReviewStatus.swift`, `Asset.swift`, `AssetRepository.swift`, `ReviewEvent.swift` (only additive post-linkage fields). Campaign's transition table, versioning, and approval semantics do not change.

- `PostRepository`: `createQuickCapture`, `saveDraft`, `applyTransition` (via `ReviewStateMachine`), `setTargets`, `schedule` (rejects unless `.approved`), `publishNowSimulated`, `publishIfDue(now:)`, `materializeNextOccurrence(postID:)`.
- `PostPublishingCoordinator` (CockpitServices): scans due posts, publishes with fake permalink + timestamp as a history event, and synchronously materializes the next recurrence at that same moment — never on a background timer.
- `ReviewEventKind` additions: `postScheduled`, `postPublished`, `postRecurred`, `autoEngagementSimulated`, `simulatedPublishingFailed`.
- `ReviewEventSnapshot`/`ReviewEventRepository`: add `postID`, `events(forPostID:)`.
- Schema container: register `Post` + attachment-link model.

**App shell:** `AppEnvironment`, `MarketingCampaignCockpitApp` (inject `PostRepository`/coordinator, run startup due-post sweep + on-`.active` sweep), `Route` (add `postList`, `postComposer`, `postDetail`), `HistoryScope` (add `.post`), `RootView` (tab shell: Campaigns, Calendar, Ideas, Analytics), `StatusHistoryViewModel` + `HistoryEntryRow` (post event labels).

**New UI (Features/Posts/):** `PostInboxView` (quick-capture + promote), `PostComposerView` (caption/label/channels/assets/preview cards; schedule controls structurally disabled until Approved; recurrence picker; "Simulated" labeling throughout), `PostCalendarView` (month grid, color-by-channel, tap-through), `PostDetailView` (edit, per-channel previews, "Publish now (Simulated)" button, auto-engagement toggles writing history events, history link), `PostAnalyticsView` (channel/label filters, mock Charts), `PostHubViewModel` + per-screen view models with empty/loading/error states.

interfaces.name: ReviewState
interfaces.signature: enum ReviewState: String, Codable, Sendable { case draft, inReview, needsRework, approved }
interfaces.notes: Canonical status machine.

interfaces.name: ReviewEventType
interfaces.signature: enum ReviewEventType: String, Codable, Sendable { case created, importVersion, submitForReview, requestRework, approve, autoRevertToDraft, deleteCampaign, deleteAsset, restoreFromTrash, restoreComplete }
interfaces.notes: Backbone of status history action typing.

interfaces.name: TrashState
interfaces.signature: enum TrashState: String, Codable, Sendable { case active, pendingDelete, deleted }
interfaces.notes: Soft-delete finite state for campaign/asset safety.

interfaces.name: Campaign
interfaces.signature: @Model final class Campaign { var id: UUID; var briefText: String; var createdAt: Date; var isTrashed: Bool; var scheduledDeletionAt: Date?; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade, inverse: \Asset.campaign) var assets: [Asset] }
interfaces.notes: Campaign-as-container invariant; SwiftData model in CockpitData package.

interfaces.name: Asset
interfaces.signature: @Model final class Asset { var id: UUID; var campaign: Campaign?; var currentVersionID: UUID; var status: ReviewStatus; var isTrashed: Bool; var scheduledDeletionAt: Date?; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade, inverse: \AssetVersion.asset) var versions: [AssetVersion]; @Relationship(deleteRule: .cascade, inverse: \ReviewEvent.asset) var events: [ReviewEvent] }
interfaces.notes: currentVersionID is the only field a new upload changes.

interfaces.name: AssetVersion
interfaces.signature: @Model final class AssetVersion { var id: UUID; var asset: Asset?; var versionNumber: Int; var relativeFilePath: String; var fileType: String; var fileSizeBytes: Int64; var createdAt: Date }
interfaces.notes: Immutable once written, file-backed only (relativeFilePath). Embedding bytes directly (Gemma3's proposal) is explicitly rejected as a regression against the locked file-backed-asset decision.

interfaces.name: ReviewEvent
interfaces.signature: @Model final class ReviewEvent { var id: UUID; var campaign: Campaign?; var asset: Asset?; var kind: ReviewEventKind; var actor: EventActor; var note: String?; var timestamp: Date }
interfaces.notes: Append-only, drives Status History; never mutated or deleted.

interfaces.name: ReviewStateMachine
interfaces.signature: protocol ReviewStateMachine { func canTransition(from: ReviewState, to: ReviewState) -> Bool; func transition(assetID: UUID, to: ReviewState, note: String?, timestamp: Date) throws -> ReviewEvent }
interfaces.notes: Owns all transition guardrails; used by services and tested in unit tests.

interfaces.name: VersioningPolicy
interfaces.signature: protocol VersioningPolicy { func nextVersionIndex(currentVersionIDs: [UUID]) -> Int; func markAsCurrent(currentID: UUID?, newVersionID: UUID) -> UUID }
interfaces.notes: Append-only policy helper, no overwrite semantics.

interfaces.name: StatusDerivation
interfaces.signature: protocol StatusDerivation { func campaignWorstStatus(assets: [Asset], eventLog: [ReviewEvent]) -> ReviewState?; func assetCurrentVersionID(_ asset: Asset) -> UUID? }
interfaces.notes: Single source for aggregate state and current-version logic.

interfaces.name: InputValidation
interfaces.signature: protocol InputValidation { func isNonEmptyTrimmed(_ text: String) -> Bool }
interfaces.notes: Reject whitespace-only brief and rework notes everywhere.

interfaces.name: AssetFileStore
interfaces.signature: protocol AssetFileStore { func copyToAppStorage(sourceURL: URL, destinationRelativePath: String) async throws -> URL; func validateFile(url: URL) -> Bool; func deleteIfExists(relativePath: String) throws; func exists(relativePath: String) -> Bool }
interfaces.notes: All binary IO in Application Support with atomic write semantics.

interfaces.name: CampaignRepository
interfaces.signature: protocol CampaignRepository: Sendable { func createCampaign(brief: String) async throws -> UUID; func updateBrief(campaignID: UUID, brief: String) async throws; func fetchCampaignList() throws -> [Campaign]; func softDeleteCampaign(id: UUID) async throws; func restoreCampaign(id: UUID) async throws }
interfaces.notes: Speaks only UUID, never PersistentIdentifier, to keep CockpitDomain SwiftData-free. Implemented as an actor in CockpitData.

interfaces.name: AssetRepository
interfaces.signature: protocol AssetRepository: Sendable { func createAsset(campaignID: UUID, initialFile: StoredFileReference) async throws -> UUID; func addVersion(assetID: UUID, campaignID: UUID, file: StoredFileReference) async throws -> UUID; func applyTransition(assetID: UUID, action: ReviewAction) async throws -> ReviewStatus; func fetchAssets(for campaignID: UUID) throws -> [Asset]; func softDeleteAsset(id: UUID) async throws; func restoreAsset(id: UUID) async throws }
interfaces.notes: applyTransition runs ReviewStateMachine.transition then persists; also responsible for auto-revert-to-Draft + system ReviewEvent when the only in-review asset is deleted.

interfaces.name: HistoryRepository
interfaces.signature: protocol HistoryRepository { func append(_ event: ReviewEvent) async throws; func fetchForCampaign(_ campaignID: UUID) throws -> [ReviewEvent]; func fetchForAsset(_ assetID: UUID) throws -> [ReviewEvent]; func purgeEvents(olderThan: Date) async throws }
interfaces.notes: Never mutates existing rows; append-only timeline source.

interfaces.name: StartupRecoveryService
interfaces.signature: protocol StartupRecoveryService { func reconcilePendingOperations(now: Date) async throws }
interfaces.notes: Cold-launch repair for imports interrupted mid-write (distinct from SoftDeleteCoordinator's deletion-finalization sweep).

interfaces.name: UndoCoordinator
interfaces.signature: protocol UndoCoordinator { func beginDeletion(id: UUID, isCampaign: Bool, expiry: Date) async throws; func undoDeletion(id: UUID, isCampaign: Bool) async throws; func isPendingDeletion(id: UUID) -> Bool }
interfaces.notes: Backs undo-toasts and survives backgrounding.

interfaces.name: ThumbnailService
interfaces.signature: protocol ThumbnailService { func makeThumbnail(for fileURL: URL, size: CGSize) async throws -> UIImage? }
interfaces.notes: Uses QLThumbnailGenerator; async cached rendering.

interfaces.name: AppRoute
interfaces.signature: enum AppRoute: Hashable { case campaignList; case campaignDetail(UUID); case assetDetail(UUID); case statusHistoryCampaign(UUID); case statusHistoryAsset(UUID) }
interfaces.notes: Navigation stack route contract.

interfaces.name: CampaignListViewState
interfaces.signature: enum CampaignListViewState { case booting; case empty; case loaded([Campaign]); case error(String) }
interfaces.notes: Mandatory UI states for root screen, per design_handoff.

interfaces.name: CampaignDetailViewState
interfaces.signature: enum CampaignDetailViewState { case loading; case noAssets; case hasAssets([Asset]); case importInProgress(UUID, progress: Double); case importFailed(UUID, String); case error(String) }
interfaces.notes: Includes inline import failure/retry path.

interfaces.name: AssetDetailViewState
interfaces.signature: enum AssetDetailViewState { case loadingVersionMetadata; case draftPreReview; case previewReady; case previewFailed(String); case reviewWait; case reworkNoteRequired; case approveConfirmPending; case undoDeleteCountdown; case removedWhileViewing; case error(String) }
interfaces.notes: The nine locked Asset Detail states from design_handoff, including the terminal removed-while-viewing case.

interfaces.name: StatusHistoryViewState
interfaces.signature: enum StatusHistoryViewState { case loading; case empty; case listing([ReviewEventSnapshot]); case error(String) }
interfaces.notes: Timeline view, no nested transitions, lowest bug surface by design.

interfaces.name: CampaignListViewModel
interfaces.signature: protocol CampaignListViewModel { var state: CampaignListViewState { get }; func onAppear() async; func createCampaign(brief: String) async; func deleteCampaign(id: UUID) async; func undoLastDelete() async; func openCampaign(_ id: UUID) }
interfaces.notes: No direct repository or ModelContext access from views.

interfaces.name: CampaignDetailViewModel
interfaces.signature: protocol CampaignDetailViewModel { var state: CampaignDetailViewState { get }; func load(id: UUID) async; func importAsset(from url: URL, mediaTypeID: String) async; func retryImport(for assetID: UUID) async }
interfaces.notes: Holds campaign scoping and import lifecycle for screen.

interfaces.name: AssetDetailViewModel
interfaces.signature: protocol AssetDetailViewModel { var state: AssetDetailViewState { get }; func load(assetID: UUID) async; func requestReview() async; func requestRework(note: String) async; func approveWithConfirmation() async; func preview(versionID: UUID?) async throws -> URL; func deleteCurrentAsset(undoSeconds: TimeInterval) async }
interfaces.notes: Controls decision actions and read-only version selection.

interfaces.name: StatusHistoryViewModel
interfaces.signature: protocol StatusHistoryViewModel { var state: StatusHistoryViewState { get }; func load(scope: HistoryScope) async; func reload() async }
interfaces.notes: Single query per scope; immutable timeline rows.

interfaces.name: ClockService
interfaces.signature: protocol ClockService { var now: Date { get } }
interfaces.notes: Injectable time source so SoftDeleteCoordinator.reconcile and undo-window math are deterministically testable.

interfaces.name: ErrorPresenter
interfaces.signature: protocol ErrorPresenter { func userVisibleError(from error: Error) -> String; func report(_ error: Error, context: String) }
interfaces.notes: Maps internal errors to retryable user states without leaking internals; backs every screen's error state copy.

interfaces.name: AppLogger
interfaces.signature: protocol AppLogger { func log(_ level: String, message: String, metadata: [String: String]?) }
interfaces.notes: OSLog-backed diagnostics only; no analytics, no remote logging, consistent with locked privacy plan.

interfaces.name: isNonEmptyTrimmed
interfaces.signature: func isNonEmptyTrimmed(_ text: String) -> Bool
interfaces.notes: Shared validation gate for brief and rework notes.

interfaces.name: ReviewStatus
interfaces.signature: enum ReviewStatus: String, Codable, Comparable { case draft, inReview, needsRework, approved }
interfaces.notes: Comparable ordering backs WorstStatusDerivation.

interfaces.name: ReviewEventKind
interfaces.signature: enum ReviewEventKind: String, Codable { case created, versionAdded, submittedForReview, approved, needsRework, autoRevertedToDraft, assetTrashed, assetRestored, campaignTrashed, campaignRestored, deletionFinalized }
interfaces.notes: Backs HistoryEntryRow icon-keyed rendering.

interfaces.name: EventActor
interfaces.signature: enum EventActor: String, Codable { case user, system }
interfaces.notes: Real field backing user-vs-system visual distinction in Status History, not inferred from kind.

interfaces.name: ReviewAction
interfaces.signature: enum ReviewAction { case submitForReview; case approve; case needsRework(note: String) }
interfaces.notes: Input to ReviewStateMachine.transition.

interfaces.name: TransitionError
interfaces.signature: enum TransitionError: Error, Equatable { case invalidTransition(from: ReviewStatus, action: ReviewAction); case emptyReworkNote }
interfaces.notes: ReviewAction equality compares cases only, ignoring associated note text.

interfaces.name: ReviewStateMachine.transition
interfaces.signature: enum ReviewStateMachine { static func transition(from status: ReviewStatus, action: ReviewAction) -> Result<ReviewStatus, TransitionError> }
interfaces.notes: Pure function, zero SwiftData/SwiftUI imports, CockpitDomain package. Single source of truth so no view re-derives transition rules.

interfaces.name: WorstStatusDerivation.worstStatus
interfaces.signature: enum WorstStatusDerivation { static func worstStatus(for statuses: [ReviewStatus]) -> ReviewStatus? }
interfaces.notes: Used by Campaign List row chip and Status History campaign rollup; nil for zero-asset campaigns.

interfaces.name: ReworkNoteValidator.isValid
interfaces.signature: enum ReworkNoteValidator { static func isValid(_ note: String) -> Bool }
interfaces.notes: Trims whitespace/newlines before checking non-empty; gates Rework submit-button enabled state and brief-field validation.

interfaces.name: ReviewEventRepository
interfaces.signature: protocol ReviewEventRepository: Sendable { func events(forCampaignID: UUID) async throws -> [ReviewEventSnapshot]; func events(forAssetID: UUID) async throws -> [ReviewEventSnapshot]; func append(_ event: ReviewEventSnapshot) async throws }
interfaces.notes: Read/append path for Status History; ordered oldest-to-newest, immutable snapshots, never mutates existing rows.

interfaces.name: ReviewEventSnapshot
interfaces.signature: struct ReviewEventSnapshot: Identifiable, Sendable { let id: UUID; let kind: ReviewEventKind; let actor: EventActor; let note: String?; let timestamp: Date }
interfaces.notes: Sendable value type crossing the repository-actor boundary into view code.

interfaces.name: AssetFileStoring
interfaces.signature: protocol AssetFileStoring: Sendable { func importFile(from sourceURL: URL, assetID: UUID, versionNumber: Int) async throws -> StoredFileReference; func absoluteURL(for relativePath: String) -> URL; func validatePresentable(relativePath: String) -> Bool; func deleteFile(at relativePath: String) async throws }
interfaces.notes: Files live in Application Support/marketing_assets, atomic temp-file-then-rename writes. validatePresentable does the synchronous existence+non-zero-size check QuickLook needs before presenting.

interfaces.name: StoredFileReference
interfaces.signature: struct StoredFileReference: Sendable { let relativePath: String; let fileType: String; let fileSizeBytes: Int64 }
interfaces.notes: Returned by AssetFileStoring.importFile, consumed by AssetRepository.addVersion/createAsset.

interfaces.name: ImportCoordinator
interfaces.signature: actor ImportCoordinator { func importAsNewAsset(sourceURL: URL, campaignID: UUID, fileStore: AssetFileStoring, repository: AssetRepository) async throws -> UUID; func importAsNewVersion(sourceURL: URL, assetID: UUID, campaignID: UUID, fileStore: AssetFileStoring, repository: AssetRepository) async throws -> UUID }
interfaces.notes: Owns file-write-succeeds-before-record-create ordering and the security-scoped resource open/close window for .fileImporter URLs. Single enforcement point, not per-call-site.

interfaces.name: ThumbnailProviding
interfaces.signature: protocol ThumbnailProviding: Sendable { func thumbnail(for relativePath: String, size: CGSize) async -> UIImage? }
interfaces.notes: Backed by QLThumbnailGenerator with an in-memory NSCache keyed by assetVersionID+size; never blocks List scroll.

interfaces.name: SoftDeleteCoordinator
interfaces.signature: actor SoftDeleteCoordinator { func markScheduledForDeletion(kind: TrashableKind, id: UUID, windowSeconds: TimeInterval) async throws; func cancelScheduledDeletion(kind: TrashableKind, id: UUID) async throws; func reconcile(now: Date) async throws -> Int }
interfaces.notes: reconcile() called on cold launch AND on scenePhase transition to .active, not just launch — closes the background/foreground undo-window gap flagged this round. Returns count finalized.

interfaces.name: TrashableKind
interfaces.signature: enum TrashableKind { case campaign, asset }
interfaces.notes: Discriminates which repository SoftDeleteCoordinator dispatches finalize-delete to.

interfaces.name: CockpitSchema
interfaces.signature: enum CockpitSchemaV1: VersionedSchema { static var versionIdentifier: Schema.Version { .init(1, 0, 0) }; static var models: [any PersistentModel.Type] { [Campaign.self, Asset.self, AssetVersion.self, ReviewEvent.self] } }; enum CockpitMigrationPlan: SchemaMigrationPlan { static var schemas: [any VersionedSchema.Type] { [CockpitSchemaV1.self] }; static var stages: [MigrationStage] { [] } }
interfaces.notes: Real (if trivial) versioned schema + empty migration plan from day one, per locked architecture review — prevents total data loss on future model changes.

interfaces.name: ImportError
interfaces.signature: enum ImportError: Error { case unsupportedFileType, corruptOrUnreadable, diskWriteFailed, securityScopeUnavailable }
interfaces.notes: Surfaces to Campaign Detail's import-failed row state.

interfaces.name: Route
interfaces.signature: enum Route: Hashable { case campaignDetail(UUID); case assetDetail(UUID); case statusHistory(HistoryScope) }
interfaces.notes: Drives the single NavigationStack path array; UUID not PersistentIdentifier to stay Hashable/Sendable-simple.

interfaces.name: HistoryScope
interfaces.signature: enum HistoryScope: Hashable { case campaign(UUID); case asset(UUID) }
interfaces.notes: Distinguishes campaign-rollup history from single-asset history.

interfaces.name: AppEnvironment
interfaces.signature: @Observable final class AppEnvironment { let campaignRepository: CampaignRepository; let assetRepository: AssetRepository; let eventRepository: ReviewEventRepository; let fileStore: AssetFileStoring; let thumbnailService: ThumbnailProviding; let softDeleteCoordinator: SoftDeleteCoordinator }
interfaces.notes: Constructed once at app launch, injected via .environment(_:); no view touches ModelContext directly.

interfaces.name: StatusChip
interfaces.signature: struct StatusChip: View { let status: ReviewStatus; var body: some View }
interfaces.notes: Single source of truth for color+icon+text+accessibility label so no call site can break the color-is-never-sole-signal rule.

interfaces.name: AccessibilityLabelFactory.label
interfaces.signature: enum AccessibilityLabelFactory { static func label(for status: ReviewStatus) -> String; static func label(versionNumber: Int, totalVersions: Int, isCurrent: Bool) -> String }
interfaces.notes: Composes explicit full-state strings ('Status: Needs Rework', 'Version 2 of 2, current') rather than relying on auto-derivation, per locked accessibility requirements.

interfaces.name: CockpitSchemaV1
interfaces.signature: enum CockpitSchemaV1: VersionedSchema { static var versionIdentifier: Schema.Version { .init(1, 0, 0) }; static var models: [any PersistentModel.Type] { [Campaign.self, Asset.self, AssetVersion.self, ReviewEvent.self] } }; enum CockpitMigrationPlan: SchemaMigrationPlan { static var schemas: [any VersionedSchema.Type] { [CockpitSchemaV1.self] }; static var stages: [MigrationStage] { [] } }
interfaces.notes: Real, if trivial, versioned schema + empty migration plan from day one, per locked ios_architecture_review — prevents total data loss on future model changes.
